summary: Four genuinely independent defect fixes, depth-1 graph, no bead consumes another's runtime output; all empty depends_on are correct. B3 touches only ClaimBead (beads.go L162-168); B2's `bd update --status open`/clear-assignee mutation has NO existing home in beads.go (verified — it is new surface), so placing it in release.go/internal/release is genuinely achievable and gives B3 sole ownership of beads.go: real merge-order preference, not a depends_on edge. B2+B4 both name mindspec_executor.go but edit different regions (B4: InitSpecWorkspace CreateBranch line L86; B2: WorktreeOps.Remove path) — no line overlap; advisory order B4-before-B2 is sound and at worst trivially mergeable. Right-sized at 4 (inside 3-5). B2 is the one substantive bead but stays a single cohesive cycle (new release.go + 6-step order + 2 RED tests + executor wiring) with no internal serial chain. Advisory merge order B1->B3->B4->B2 is sound.
notes: Verified seams all exist as the plan claims: SelectWork items[0] fallthrough (select.go L27), CheckDirtyTree (guard.go L110), complete.go os.Chdir(root) pattern (L78), gitutil execCommand/rejectOptionLike/HasRemote (gitops.go L22/L63/L225), runBDCombFn/runBDFn/swapExec test seams, newRepoExecutor + fakeWorktreeOps.

Minor (non-blocking) precision gap: B2's changed-files note says it must 'expose the bead-worktree removal through WorktreeOps.Remove' in the executor, but WorktreeOps.Remove ALREADY exists (interface L29, default impl L38). The real B2 executor work is reaching WorktreeOps from cmd code, since newExecutor() returns the executor.Executor INTERFACE and WorktreeOps lives only on concrete *MindspecExecutor (the spec AC testability note flags this). B2 may therefore add a worktree-removal method to the Executor interface OR construct/assert the concrete executor in release.go. Either path is still a different region from B4's L86 edit, so the merge-order classification holds; this is a wording sharpening for the B2 impl prompt, not a decomposition defect.
